raw:
{
  "weights": {
    "R1.1": 0.3,
    "R1.2": 0.7,
    "R1.3": 0.7,
    "R1.4": 0.7,
    "R1.5": 0.0,
    "R1.6": 0.3,
    "R1.7": 0.7,
    "RX.1": 1.0,
    "RX.2": 1.0,
    "RX.3": 1.0,
    "RX.4": 1.0,
    "RX.5": 1.0
  },
  "active_set": ["R1.2", "R1.3", "R1.4", "R1.7", "RX.1", "RX.2", "RX.3", "RX.4", "RX.5"],
  "rationale": {
    "R1.2": "The fragment contains internal tensions — training on 6 languages but evaluating on 5; claiming 'first' classifier while crediting Smith et al. (2022) for the training procedure and Liu et al. (2019) for the core approach — that logical-consistency review would need to resolve.",
    "R1.3": "The headline claim of 'competitive performance across both high-resource and low-resource languages' is only partially evidenced; two languages (Spanish, Mandarin) are shown inline while low-resource-language results are entirely deferred to an unseen Table 4.",
    "R1.4": "The 'first cross-lingual sentiment classifier' assertion is in direct tension with the cited prior work; the actual delta SentiBridge contributes over Smith et al. and Liu et al. is underspecified and requires scoping.",
    "R1.7": "Smith et al. (2022) is attributed as the source of the novel training procedure, yet SentiBridge claims novelty for the same procedure; Liu et al. (2019) is cited for the direct-on-target-language idea. Attribution boundaries need verification."
  },
  "clarification_needed": true,
  "clarification_question": "Should I check whether the claims are substantively sound and evidence-backed (a logical/empirical critique surfacing gaps like the low-resource evaluation hole and the novelty tension with cited prior work), or check the writing quality and clarity (grammar, phrasing, and presentation polish)?"
}

rationale.R1.2: The fragment contains internal tensions — training on 6 languages but evaluating on 5; claiming 'first' classifier while crediting Smith et al. (2022) for the training procedure and Liu et al. (2019) for the core approach — that logical-consistency review would need to resolve.
rationale.R1.3: The headline claim of 'competitive performance across both high-resource and low-resource languages' is only partially evidenced; two languages (Spanish, Mandarin) are shown inline while low-resource-language results are entirely deferred to an unseen Table 4.
rationale.R1.4: The 'first cross-lingual sentiment classifier' assertion is in direct tension with the cited prior work; the actual delta SentiBridge contributes over Smith et al. and Liu et al. is underspecified and requires scoping.
rationale.R1.7: Smith et al. (2022) is attributed as the source of the novel training procedure, yet SentiBridge claims novelty for the same procedure; Liu et al. (2019) is cited for the direct-on-target-language idea. Attribution boundaries need verification.

parsed.clarification_question: Should I check whether the claims are substantively sound and evidence-backed (a logical/empirical critique surfacing gaps like the low-resource evaluation hole and the novelty tension with cited prior work), or check the writing quality and clarity (grammar, phrasing, and presentation polish)?
